data_in.description: Negative-mode MS/MS spectra with annotated molecular formulas and adduct assignments from a public dataset (MassIVE or MetaboLights)
Pretrained MIST-CF model checkpoint with positive-mode formula transformer weights

data_out.description: Fine-tuned MIST-CF model checkpoint with negative-mode adduct support
Top-k formula ranking accuracy metrics (top-1, top-5, top-10) on negative-mode test set, reported as accuracy_metrics.csv

domain_knowledge: Negative-mode ionization produces characteristic adducts ([M-H]−, [M+Cl]−, [M+FA]−) with distinct m/z shifts that differ fundamentally from positive-mode adducts and require separate training to be recognized by neural models.

Formula transformers learn data-dependent fragmentation patterns rather than relying on physicochemical fragmentation rules, so negative-mode spectra exhibit different fragmentation signatures requiring domain-specific retraining.
Top-k accuracy (top-1, top-5, top-10) is the standard ranking metric for de novo chemical formula assignment and must be computed on a held-out test set that is independent of the fine-tuning set.
Sinusoidal formula embeddings (SCARF) encode molecular structure as continuous vectors; this representation is mode-agnostic and need not be retrained, only the adduct-aware output layers.

Public MS/MS repositories (MassIVE, MetaboLights) require stringent filtering for high-quality negative-mode entries with unambiguous formula and adduct annotations to avoid training-set contamination.

direct_checks: verify that the GitHub repository samgoldman97/mist-cf contains a MULTI_ADDUCT_SUPPORT module or component with code comments or documentation explicitly listing supported adduct types
verify file_exists: a trained or fine-tuned model checkpoint in the repository targeting negative-mode MS/MS spectra
verify that the repository contains a script or notebook that can load a public negative-mode MS/MS dataset (e.g. from MassIVE, MetaboLights, or PRIDE with explicit accession or URL)
verify that evaluation results (top-k formula ranking accuracy metrics) are reported in a table, CSV, or JSON file within the repository with numeric accuracy values and corresponding k values
verify format_is: the accuracy results are in a structured format (table, JSON, or CSV) with at least columns/fields for 'k', 'accuracy', and 'dataset_name' or 'mode'
verify contains_substring: any model card, README, or results document references 'negative' mode or '[M-H]-' adduct support

verify output_matches_reference: if a reference negative-mode benchmark is cited, the reported top-k accuracies fall within the expected range stated in that benchmark (requires expert review of literature values)
expert_review: chemical plausibility of the negative-mode adduct list: confirm that selected adducts ([M-H]-, [M+Cl]-, [M+HCOO]-, etc.) represent the most common species observed in negative-mode ESI-MS/MS for organic small molecules

appropriateness of fine-tuning strategy: assess whether the retraining approach (learning rates, batch sizes, data split, validation protocol) is sound for transfer learning from positive to negative mode

statistical significance of top-k accuracy: confirm that reported accuracies are computed on a held-out test set with sufficient sample size and that confidence intervals or standard errors are provided
completeness of negative-mode MS/MS dataset: verify that the public dataset used contains sufficient structural diversity and spectral quality to support robust model training and generalization
comparison fairness: if baseline or ablation comparisons are made, confirm that all models are trained and evaluated on identical data splits and with equivalent hyperparameter tuning effort

Preprocess and normalize spectra, filtering by quality thresholds.
Extend MIST-CF's adduct layer to tokenize common negative-mode adducts ([M-H]−, [M+Cl]−, [M+FA]−).

Evaluate on held-out negative-mode test set and compute top-k ranking accuracy.

task_description: Extend MIST-CF's MULTI_ADDUCT_SUPPORT to handle negative-mode adducts (e.g., [M-H]−) by fine-tuning the formula transformer on negative-mode MS/MS spectra from a public dataset, and report top-k formula ranking accuracy metrics.

task_objective: Extend MIST-CF's adduct support to negative-mode adducts, retrain on negative-mode spectra, and evaluate top-k formula ranking accuracy.

title: Extend MIST-CF multi-adduct support to negative ionisation mode
uncertainty_notes: This card was generated by the LLM-assisted pipeline and needs scientific expert review.
Each TracedClaim's evidence_span has been substring-checked against its source section; see logs/llm_calls.jsonl and capsules/<task_id>/quality_report.json for groundedness results.
workflow_description: 1. Acquire negative-mode MS/MS spectra from a public metabolomics repository (e.g., MassIVE, MetaboLights) with annotated molecular formulas and negative adducts. 2. Preprocess spectra by normalizing intensity and removing noise below a defined threshold. 3. Extend the adduct tokenization layer in MIST-CF to include common negative-mode adducts ([M-H]−, [M+Cl]−, [M+FA]−). 4. Fine-tune the formula transformer neural network using negative-mode spectra, maintaining the existing sinusoidal formula embeddings from SCARF. 5. Evaluate the fine-tuned model on a held-out negative-mode test set by computing top-1, top-5, and top-10 formula ranking accuracy. 6. Compile results in a metrics table comparing performance across negative adduct types.
